name: San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its viewpoints
name_original: San Juan de Gaztelugatxe y sus miradores
region_intro: Jogging around Bakio offers diverse terrain, from extensive coastal paths to challenging mountain trails. The region features over 6 kilometers of coastline, including Bakio Beach, providing flat surfaces with sea views. Impressive cliffs lead towards San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, offering elevated routes and dramatic scenery. Bakio is also nestled within a valley bordered by mountains like Jata, providing opportunities for trail running through forests and traditional txakoli vineyards. Best jogging routes around Bakio The most popular jogging route is San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its viewpoints, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes about 47 minutes to complete, offering views of the iconic hermitage. Another top favourite among local runners is Matxitxako Viewpoint – Matxitxako Lighthouse loop from Gaztelugatxeko Doniene / San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, a moderate 4.7 miles (7.6 km) path. This route provides expansive coastal vistas, including the Matxitxako Lighthouse. Local runners also love the Eneperi Restaurant – Marine Memorial Sculpture loop from Bakio, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes and viewpoints, often completed in about 1 hour 16 minutes. Jogging around Bakio is defined by extensive coastlines, dramatic cliffs, and a mountainous backdrop. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from easy beach runs to more challenging trail running. The routes in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Bakio's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Bakio?
A: Bakio offers a diverse selection of &lt;strong&gt;15 running routes&lt;/strong&gt;, catering to various preferences and fitness levels. These paths explore everything from the extensive coastline to challenging mountain trails.

Q: Are there easy jogging paths suitable for beginners in Bakio?
A: Yes, Bakio has options for beginners. The town's extensive coastline, including Bakio Beach, provides ideal, level surfaces for an easy jog with uninterrupted sea views. While most routes are moderate to difficult, you can find gentler sections along the promenade or explore the one easy route available on komoot.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see along the running trails in Bakio?
A: Jogging routes in Bakio showcase a stunning array of natural features. You'll encounter dramatic cliffs, especially those leading towards San Juan de Gaztelugatxe, offering breathtaking scenery. The region is also nestled within a valley bordered by mountains like Jata, Burgoa, and Garbola, providing opportunities to run through lush forests and traditional txakoli vineyards. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jata Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers rewarding panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Bakio?
A: Yes, Bakio features several excellent circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40319909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Viewpoint – Matxitxako Lighthouse loop from Gaztelugatxeko Doniene / San Juan de Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.7-mile (7.6 km) path offering expansive coastal vistas. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20503860" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bakio Beach – Hermitage of San Pelayo de Bakio loop from Bakio&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 4.3 miles (6.9 km) long.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can discover while running in Bakio?
A: Bakio's running routes are rich with landmarks and viewpoints. You can run towards the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/275684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint John of Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, a protected biotope. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058607793/san-juan-de-gaztelugatxe-and-its-viewpoints" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Gaztelugatxe and its viewpoints&lt;/a&gt; route offers stunning views of the hermitage. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/527262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; also provides an expansive 270-degree view of the coast, accessible via routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40319909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Matxitxako Viewpoint – Matxitxako Lighthouse loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Bakio?
A: The running trails in Bakio are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.6 stars from over 16 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from the beautiful coastal paths with sea views to the challenging mountain trails that offer rewarding panoramic vistas of the coastline and surrounding area.

Q: Are there any longer distance running trails available in Bakio?
A: For those seeking a longer challenge, Bakio offers several routes, including 7 difficult trails. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31903305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de Gaztelugatxe – Matxitxako Lighthouse loop from Gaztelugatxeko Doniene / San Juan de Gaztelugatxe&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 9.4 miles (15.2 km) and features significant elevation changes, making it a demanding yet rewarding run.

Q: What is the best season to go jogging in Bakio?
A: Bakio's unique microclimate, favorable for grape cultivation, suggests pleasant conditions for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the varied terrain means you can likely find suitable routes year-round. Coastal paths are great in warmer months, while mountain trails might be more enjoyable in cooler weather, offering a refreshing escape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly jogging routes in Bakio?
A: For families, the flat coastal paths and the promenade along Bakio Beach are excellent choices. These areas provide a safe and accessible environment for a leisurely jog or a stroll with children, offering beautiful sea views without significant elevation changes. While specific 'family-friendly' routes are not explicitly listed, the beach area is generally suitable.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails in Bakio?
A: While specific parking locations for each trail are not detailed, Bakio is a town that supports outdoor activities. Given its popularity for hiking and running, you can typically find parking near popular starting points like Bakio Beach or areas close to the access points for San Juan de Gaztelugatxe. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to eat near the running routes in Bakio?
A: Yes, Bakio is a vibrant coastal town, and you'll find various cafes and restaurants, especially along the promenade and in the town center. After your run, you can enjoy local cuisine or a refreshing drink. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31873974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eneperi Restaurant – Marine Memorial Sculpture loop from Bakio&lt;/a&gt; even starts near a restaurant, suggesting convenient options for post-run refreshments.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the running trails in Bakio?
A: The running trails in Bakio offer a range of difficulty levels. Out of the 15 routes, there is &lt;strong&gt;1 easy route&lt;/strong&gt;, &lt;strong&gt;7 moderate routes&lt;/strong&gt;, and &lt;strong&gt;7 difficult routes&lt;/strong&gt;. This variety ensures that both casual joggers and experienced trail runners can find suitable challenges, from flat coastal runs to demanding mountain ascents.
tips: Islet of the Biscayan town of Bermeo. It is very peculiar in that the island is connected to the mainland by a two-arched bridge. On the island there is a hermitage dedicated to San Juan that dates from the 10th century, although some discoveries date from the 9th century.
